strengths: Excellent insight-driven title that perfectly mimics the reference deck's numbering and 'so-what' style.
Flawless execution of structural elements, including the title divider line, source formatting, and footer.
Correct typography pairing (serif for the main title, sans-serif for body text).
weaknesses: The pastel color palette (pink, light blue, light green) used for the table columns is uncharacteristic of McKinsey's standard restrained blue/grey style.
Full-column background shading is a bit heavy; McKinsey typically uses subtle row shading or white backgrounds with clean divider lines.
gemini_score.one_line_verdict: A highly professional slide with perfect structural replication, slightly let down by an overly colorful, non-standard pastel table palette.
